expect: Domain-global linkage >=15% at N=931 (was 10.1% at S429). >=2 global frontiers have domain evidence from recent DOMEX lanes. Hub-fraction baseline measured.
actual: Domain-global linkage 16.8% at S436 baseline (pre-crosslink), 28.3% after applying 41 threshold-6 crosslinks. Hub-fraction (top-3/total): 9.9% (278/2817 edges) — well below 20% monoculture threshold. L-601 dominance: 196/2817 (7.0%). K_avg=3.016 at N=933. Global-to-domain links: 5/12 (41.7%) — DOWN from 75% at S429. Divergent pattern: domain→global UP, global→domain DOWN.
diff: Linkage prediction (>=15%) CONFIRMED at 16.8% baseline (+6.7pp from S429). Hub-fraction safely below 20% — L-1012's monoculture concern is premature. Key surprise: global→domain links DECLINED (75%→41.7%) despite domain→global INCREASE. Root cause: domain frontier resolution removes links faster than new crosslinks are added. Crosslink application (threshold-6) pushed domain→global from 16.8%→28.3% in-session.

three_mechanism_status.M1_structural: 28.3% linkage (up from 10.1% at S429). frontier_crosslink.py effective when re-run.
three_mechanism_status.M3_routing: STILL MISSING — no historian sessions explicitly targeting global frontier advancement.
